I had a wonderful experience with King Law Firm and I want to give special thanks to Miss Julie at the front desk. From my very first call, she was warm, professional, and incredibly helpful. Miss Julie went above and beyond to answer my questions and make sure I was connected with the right person. Her kindness and efficiency made the whole experience smooth and stress-free. She’s truly an asset to the firm — thank you, Miss Julie!

I contacted King Law Firm in Ocala last Friday to retain them for my accident case after explaining that my situation had evolved beyond a standard personal injury claim and I was no longer comfortable with the original firm I had contracted. They initially seemed willing to take my case and even advised me to terminate my current representation before signing with them — which I did in good faith.

After that, communication fell apart. It took them all day Monday to send a contract, which turned out to limit representation only to “personal injury” despite my clear explanation in Friday that the case involves more. I raised that concern immediately, but for the next two days I was repeatedly put off and told “we’ll get back to you” while they ignored a simple, critical question that determined whether I’d even have legal coverage.

I have been left without representation due to their lack of responsiveness and organization. If this is how they handle clients before a case even starts, I can’t imagine how they’d handle something complex. I wouldn’t recommend this firm to anyone who values communication, follow-through, or professionalism.

reviews.text:
If you are in Ocala and dealing with an injunction or any criminal defense issue, hiring this attorney is the smartest decision you can make. These cases can destroy your reputation, your freedom, your ability to work, and your future — and you do not get a second chance to handle them correctly.

From the first call, it was obvious we were

dealing with someone operating at a completely different level than most attorneys. He was sharp, aggressive when needed, incredibly prepared, and treated the situation with the seriousness it deserves.

Injunction cases move fast, the stakes are high, and most people have no idea what they’re walking into until it’s too late. He knew exactly how to navigate the court process in Marion County, how to challenge the allegations, and how to protect my rights every step of the way.

In court, he was confident, strategic, and clearly respected. The outcome could not have gone better, and I genuinely believe that without his representation, things could have gone very differently.

If you choose anyone else in Ocala for injunction defense, you are taking an unnecessary risk. Do yourself a favor and call him first. Not hiring him would honestly be foolish.

Highly, highly recommended.

reviews.text:
As a passionate, educated professional with a doctorate degree, my experience with this company was extremely poor. I would like to advise anyone who is considering in hiring this company, to reconsider your decision especially, if you have limited funds to hire an attorney. I would also like to mention, this review is to educate and bring awareness to those in the Ocala, Daytona and rural areas who may be vulnerable to greedy companies like Defend Ocala, while also being extremely understaffed.

Jeff requested $4,000 to work on my family member’s case based on the charges he researched. Upon paying, he requested an additional $2,000 a day later, AFTER we already agreed on the final amount, claiming he would need the additional funds for the extra victim in the case, which was not true. I requested him to send this in writing, explaining the reasoning for the additional cost before I would send anything else. Now, if you go to their website, the mission statement states you will pay a fixed flat fee which will cover everything from beginning to end of the case. However, they can mysteriously request additional funds for work that hasn’t been worked on yet.

On day 3, I received a call from Brett introducing himself and claims he would be working on the case moving forward and if I had any questions or concerns to contact him or his assistant , Dawn. The very next day, I contacted Brett and through out the entire week on multiple occasions. No response. I reached out to Dawn who initially tells me she works in family law and is learning on the job to cover someone who is on maternity leave. Dawn couldn’t answer any of my questions correctly. Instead of forwarding my questions and concerns to Brett, she decided to give me misleading information in regards to my family members case. Also, I had to assist her to look up the case because she was having difficulties looking any up. Now if you didn’t know, family & criminal law are 2 distinct legal processes. I mean the level of professionalism and communication was not there. Therefore, I decided to take matters into my own hands and get a second opinion else where. After 2 weeks of hell, unanswered questions from Brett, misleading information from Dawn and money hungry Jeff, this led me to requesting for a refund of the retainer paid. Jeff denied the refund of the difference and couldn’t provide any documentation of work that was done so I could forward to the new attorney. And the reason I requested a refund for the difference was because I requested Jeff to show up via zoom to the first appearance which was on the same day I hired him.

Thankfully, I was able to hire another attorney who not only was trustworthy from beginning to end but also answered ALL my questions and concerns, was extremely passionate about their career AND charged less AND was able to get all charges dropped in less than 3 weeks. I can also forward the new attorney’s information if needed.

Hopefully, this information/perspective was useful for the next client that could be possibly fall victim to misleading and untrustworthy firms as such.
reviews.ownerResponse:
At DefendOcala, we provide top-notch defense for a fixed, flat fee. We always have. And we've provided refunds & forgiven fees when they have been warranted. That's just the level of service we provide.

But some clients can be difficult or don't listen to the info we give them. This is one of those times. It can be exhausting, but we remain committed to providing impeccable legal defense to the good people of Marion County.

The person who wrote this review is lying to you. I brought the reciepts to prove it. Check the photos section of this Google business page for screenshots of the text messages she sent me.

For those interested in the truth, here's what really happened:

1. Ashley called on a Friday monring. Her brother was arrested in Tallahassee on a Marion County warrant and being held with no bond. He was supposed to walk at his college graduation that evening & Ashley and her family were panicked. We agreed to take the case & appear at his Tallahassee first appearance for a non refundable fee of $4000. Ashley agreed to the fee being non refundable.

2. Ashley appeared with me at the first appearance. That is when we learned of a second case. Evidently, Ashley's brother was accused of scamming multiple people's bank accounts on multiple occasions. More cases means more evidence to review, more victims to deal with, and more work for the lawyer. Therefore, a second case means an additional fee. That morning I got the police reports for Ashley before they were publicly available. She saw for herself there were multiple cases & more work. For some reason, she left that info out of her review.

3. After first appearance, I spoke with Ashley about the second case & offered a discounted fee and multiple payment options. She didn't have to hire us to handle the second case if she didn't want to, but she and the family agreed to pay the additional $2000 for us to handle the second case.

4. More specifically, that evening Ashley texted me, "Can you please start working to put the agreement together over the weekend. We are moving forward with the $2000. Thank." You can see this text in the photos on our Google business page. Only Ashley can say why she left this important info out of her review.

5. Ashley complains about the additional fee now, but she agreed to it & going to court is not like going to Publix. There are no BOGOs. Ashley knows she wouldn't do two jobs for the price of one. But for some reason, after initially agreeing to the fee for the second case, she now expects us to do the additional work for free.

6. After the first appearance, Ashley and the family were informed that her brother's case would go through a quiet period with little to no news. Transfer to Marion County could take up to 30 days & the State could take 2-4 weeks to deliver discovery to us.

7. Nevertheless, on Day 3 Brett contacted Ashley. Day 3 was a Sunday which meant Brett contacted her on a weekend. That is not surprising. Brett is a conscientous lawyer with a very kind and patient disposition.

8. Ashley says in her review that she contacted Brett's office & got no response. But our records show that, in additon to speaking on Day 3, Brett spoke with Ashley on Day 4, Day 6, and Day 7. Each of those calls lasted between 6 and 24 minutes. For reference, her brother's first court date wasn't until Day 36.

9. Furthermore, I was texted daily by another family member. At this point, to say there was no commnication is plainly and provably false. We went above & beyond any reasonable expectation of communication with this family throughout the time we had the cases and were prepared to continue to do so.

10. Finally, it's true that the cases were recently dismissed....for lack of jurisdiction. That's not the result of some grand legal manuvering. It's because a prosecutor looked at a map.

We're disappointed to see Ashley handle things this way, but we stand by our work, our policies, and our communication in this case. Thank you.

reviews.ownerResponse:
So good to hear from you, Aurora, but you are not our client.
For those reading this review, Aurora called and emailed after 5 pm on a Friday evening & is upset that her call was not returned by 10 AM on Saturday morning. Staff members at DefendOcala have spoken with Aurora multiple times. Aurora claims to be the sister of a client who is currently in the Marion County Jail. Aurora is one of at least 3 people claiming to be family members of this client and all of them have been informed *several* times in *multiple* phone calls with *multiple* members of our staff (including myself) that the client's next court date isn't until February 19.
To minmize miscommunication and repetitive phone calls, we requested that *one* person from the family be selected as the point of contact, yet we continue to get *multiple* calls from *multiple* family members. This includes calls and emails after hours and on weekends (such as the one that prompted this review), despite the fact that the family has been told multiple times the status of the client's cases and that her next court date isn't for almost 3 weeks.
Perhaps you are new to the court system, Aurora, so let me explain this to you in writing since you didn't get it the last time we spoke on the phone- Your alleged family member is in jail because she missed court before she hired us & the court put out a warrant for her arrest. Her case is set for court on February 19. That date is set by the clerk of the court, not by us. If you call the clerk after hours on a Friday, you will not get a response by Saturday morning. This is because the courts are CLOSED on the weekends. This includes judges, prosecutors, and clerks taking time in the evenings & on the weekends to handle personal business and care for their families.
We at DefendOcala also have families and need to take that time, as well. Nevertheless, we still regularly squeeze in work on the weekends. In fact, your family member's lead attorney had a video meeting with a client at 10:15 this Saturday morning.
Additionally, like many business owners, I also regularly work throughout the weekends. But over the years I've learned that it is best to try to give myself a 24-hour weekend break (or at least a full Saturday morning) to rest and recharge, so we can continue providing the excellent legal representation that we are known for & that our clients deserve.
This fairly nominal break is designed to not only help us stay sharp, but is specifically taken on the weekends because we know that - no matter what you have to say in your Friday night call - absolutely nothing can be done over the weekend. Why? Because, as stated above, the courts are CLOSED on the weekends.
Turning to your specific complaint, because we have spoken to you and other family members multiple times in recent days, and because the courts are closed for the weekend, there is absolutely no earthly reason for us to return a call to you after hours on a Friday night to go over the same issues you and other family members have raised on every other call. With your family member being in custody for the last couple of weeks, there is no chance of there being any new information that you could offer on her case that hasn't already been disucssed. Therefore, there is no reason for us to violate our own boundaries & interrupt the small portion of the weekend that we try to keep for ourselves to return a call that you did not make until after hours on a Friday evening.
If you want to be given respect and have your calls returned promptly, I would request that you show the same respect by respecting people's time, taking into account the fact that - like everyone else - attorneys have lives outside of work, and listening to what you are told when we do speak to you. If you can do that, it will save everyone a lot of frustration. Enjoy the rest of your weekend.
Thank you for the review!

Just the other day we were notified by Medicaid that we needed to do something and had to provide new legal documents in four working days. Cannot thank them enough or recommend them more highly! They are worth every penny that they charge because they have made it where my mom can live at assisted living instead of having nothing and going to a nursing home.
They are caring, efficient and fast. You can definitely count on this firm.

Ashlee was very responsive and timely with paperwork. Susie made the process so seamless and made me feel not alone, I'm forever grateful for that in our grieving process. Thanks, everybody at Absolute Law! You're the best
very knowledgeable and eager to help you

Shame on any practice that preys on potential clients in this way.

reviews.text:
Several months back I went on a holiday to Mexico.  When I came back through customs I was quite shocked to be pulled out and told I had a felony warrant from thirty years ago.  Honestly and earnestly I couldn't remember the details that caused the warrant to be written.

I'm an old man now often in a wheelchair with many health issues.  So you can imagine that I did not want this old case to chase me to the end of my life.  I live in California and didn't know anybody in the Gainesville, FL region in which this warrant was written.

I found Andy Ingram and he gave me a phone consultation as to the particulars to my case.  He explained to me that the only way to deal with this warrant would be that I would have to surrender myself back in Florida, which I was fully prepared to do and accept personal responsibility but I must confess I had hoped he'd be able to resolve it without that outcome.  I also must confess he under charged me.  I feel as if what he actually had to do to resolve the case should have cost me more money than he charged me.

After several months he actually resolved the case for me in such a fashion that I did not have to surrender myself and he got the warrant removed and the case dismissed.  While any criminal defendant wants that outcome, it's not always possible or even reasonable.  If you find yourself having made a mistake or even being innocent you need to have an attorney who is a man of his word.  I offered Andy more money because I felt strong that he under charged me and he refused to accept one dollar more than what we agreed to initially.

So here it is, I found an attorney who gave me a very reasonable rate, honored his word, kept his pledge, gave a damn, and dispensed my case in the most beneficial way to me personally.

If you wind up with a lousy attorney it's because you didn't hire this guy.  I believe I was innocent of the criminal charge, as I tried to recall I think there was more to the story than the simple facts as it appeared.  These circumstances can always be complex.  But the simple solution is to get an attorney who has a good command of the law and who is a man of his word.

Attorney Andy Durham handled my case and I want to personally broadcast in short and long how he is hands down the best decision one can make when facing legal action.

In short:
He and his staff and professional, attentive, and caring beyond anything I have seen. Listens, prices fairly, and KEEPS IT HONEST!!! He got results. Got a complete dismissal!

The long:
Right before Christmas I had some run in with the law and I found myself facing criminal charges. My case as many others was not black and white and most attorneys were telling me that I should just take the plea. It sounded like a good deal especially when it was echoed by a detective and a social worker I knew. They threw in the whole “adjudication withheld” and it won’t matter and just accept the punishment. But this was more. It did matter and I was being given bad advice that could have detrimental affect in the future. For me it was the premise and the spirit of the law. Mr Ingram saw that and immediately asked for relevant facts. He listened. No interruptions, no tangents, just listen to it all. He told me the reality of what I’m facing and he fought for me. I believe the law is there to protect us and that it is make to create a better society but it needs representation of facts. RELEVANT FACTS that are often not shown on paper. He took to work immediately even though I retained him after arraignment and 2 days before the next court date he immediately filed for representation and started building the defense. In the span of a week I went from potentially losing my job and risking complete familial instability to having my case COMPLETELY AND UNCONDITIONALLY DISMISSED. NO STIPULATIONS. NO FEES. NO PREREQUISITES! Beyond a doubt the LITERAL best outcome is what he won when all others I consulted did not even want to try.

I can honestly say I will never look elsewhere for legal help. He and his firm has earned my loyalty and my trust. The reviews are real. This attorney is the real deal. I work with attorneys in my line of work daily and I don’t see this level of personal commitment from most. You can consult around and waste your time like I did to get peace of mind but take mine and all the other reviews to heart when I say you will not regret hiring Mr Ingram

Highly recommend them!

Vanderlaan & Vanderlaan, P.A.

reviews.text: I had high hopes when I first met Vanderlann. He spoke with grace and dignity! I trusted him fully. During my divorce preceding, things changed drastically! I began to wonder who side was he on. I was very dissatisfied! My husband got everything...our house, vehicles, money, mistress, my money, and everything else! I have been struggling 😩 ever since. My ex-husband and I had been married for 37.5 years, and he was the one who committed the infidelity in our marriage! I only got 1250 per month at first, then 1550. I have been renting ever since the  divorce! I still owe this attorney money that I can't pay. I have worked very hard all of my days! We have 3 daughters. Thank God they were all over 18 during our divorce! My divorce was completed  in 2018, but I  never left a review.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Dear Ms. Robinson:
I am writing in response to your review of our law firm on Google. I would like to begin by expressing my sincere regret for the difficult experience you had during your divorce proceedings. Divorce cases can be emotionally challenging, and I understand how important it is for clients to feel supported and adequately represented throughout the process.

I want to assure you that my primary objective as an attorney is to advocate for my clients and protect their interests. It is disheartening to hear that you feel your ex-husband received an unfair settlement and that you “wonder” whose side I was representing when my primary goal was always zealously advocating for you as my client. I wish you would have brought this to my attention in 2018 when our firm was representing you before a settlement was reached.

It is crucial to note that divorce settlements involve a complex assessment of various factors, including property division, financial resources, and child custody arrangements, among others. While the ultimate outcome may not always align with our clients' initial expectations, it is my responsibility to navigate the legal process diligently and to provide sound advice based on the facts and circumstances of each case and the application of the law to those facts. Florida is a “no-fault” divorce state, so unfortunately the court is limited on considering infidelity unless marital funds are being used by the opposing party to fund the infidelity.

I am genuinely sorry to hear that you are experiencing financial difficulties and still have outstanding legal fees. I would like to extend an invitation for you to reach out to my office at (352) 789-6744 so that we can discuss possible options for resolving this matter. It is important to me that we address your concerns and find a solution that is fair and reasonable for both parties involved.

Client satisfaction is a top priority for our firm, and we take feedback seriously as an opportunity for growth and improvement. Thank you for bringing your concerns to our attention, and I hope we can find a resolution that helps alleviate some of the challenges you have faced since the divorce.

Vanderlaan & Vanderlaan, P.A.

He explains things thoroughly and in layman’s terms.  His honesty and compassion make me feel at ease.  I give him a five-star rating and highly recommend his services.
reviews.ownerResponse: John, it is always a pleasure to work with you.  Thank you for taking the time to write the review, and trusting the DA Firm, PA with your issue.  If you need us in the future we are here for you and all the people of Ocala.

reviews.text: When I was looking for a bankruptcy attorney, Damien Aranguren came highly recommended by a friend. The DA Firm is located in Ocala, which is a bit far from me, but it was well worth the consultation, which changed my world. Damien was very kind, yet professional and straightforward. He explained my options, reassured me about my decisions, and was always available to answer the countless questions I had during one of the most difficult times of my life. I recommend him a million times over. Damien is incredibly astute and skilled when it comes to bankruptcy law. He's not just a good lawyer; he's the right lawyer.
reviews.ownerResponse: Eden, thank you for taking the time to write this review.  It really means a lot to us here at the DA Firm, PA, to hear that a customer is satisfied with our services.  Especially, when we hear that we helped someone during a tough time in their life.  The part I enjoy the most about helping people through bankruptcy is that I can see the weight lifted off their shoulders by the time my representation comes to a close. best wishes to you and your family, and thank you again for taking the time to write this review.

reviews.text: Damien is the best lawyer I have ever worked with.  His communication, relatability, and honesty is second to none.  I love that he is bilingual and can speak clear English and Spanish which is rare to see.  If you are looking for someone who cares and someone who is going give  you their full attention please call and come see Damien.  God bless
reviews.ownerResponse: It was a pleasure working with you, Mr. Rodriguez! We here at The DA Firm, PA will continue to work hard for you and all the citizens of Ocala, Florida. Please contact us if you need anything else!

reviews.text: The DA firm is awesome, Mr Aranguren was super helpful and professional. He answered any questions that I had. Thank you for your help. Will be recommending him to my friends and family.
reviews.ownerResponse: We here at The DA Firm, PA are thrilled to hear great feedback, thank you for trusting us to help you!

reviews.text: Damien Aranguren and his legal team were extraordinary throughout this entire ordeal. Being that this is my first time dealing with family court they helped make the process effortless on my end and answered all my questions without hesitation and in complete confidence. To say I felt like I was more then a client would be cliche, but there truly is no one else I would like by my side through such a mindful hassle. Even after my case was settled they made sure to help me understand the findings and work towards a positive future for myself and my family. Lessons learned in life.
reviews.ownerResponse: Christian, thank you so much for taking the time to review my services.  I always strive to put myself in my clients shoes and understand that while it may be my 100th time dealing with the same legal issue, it is most likely their first.  So I try to be thorough in answering their questions and try not to let staff, or worst, the internet, be their guide.  I try to make sure that they feel like they have an attorney that knows their case, and cares about their outcome.  It is really great to hear that you felt that way, and that you felt that strongly enough to share your experience with others.  Thanks again Damien.

reviews.text: Very professional, they helped me manage household issues and business situations. They are always ready to assist me with any endeavor, whether it's a quick call to discuss ideas or preparing legal matters for my business or family. I recommend them 100%.
reviews.ownerResponse: Edwin, thank you for the review; it's always a pleasure working with you. Thank you for answering my basic questions about my website. I hope to have a client who needs your expertise so I can repay you for your help and trust in me and my legal services. Damien

reviews.text: I have been in the legal industry for over a decade and have worked for many attorneys- both good and bad. During that time I had the pleasure of working with Damien and I can tell you that he is most definitely one of the good ones. He makes his clients feel at ease no matter what they are going through. He understands that people go through tough times in their life and he is always sympathetic to each individual situation. He is a trustworthy attorney who actually cares for his clients! I would highly recommend sitting down for a free consultation with Damien. You will not be disappointed.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Megan, honestly this review means a lot to me, you are an awesome paralegal and am so happy I had the chance to work with you. Your attention to detail and compassion was exemplary, so if you saw the qualities in me, you wrote about in your review, I must be doing something right. I always strive to be the same person to my clients and colleagues, I try to treat everyone the way I would want to be treated.

Everyone has a story and sometimes in a business that story just gets lost, by getting shuffled in to the list of case numbers. I try to remember that all my clients are facing real issues, and they are trusting me to see them through. I'm glad that the people I work with also see that effort, and when they have a great work ethic and drive, as you do, it carries a little more weight.

I wish all the best for you, your family, and your career, you are truly the best.

reviews.text:
I was about to lose my house in 2012 and I had lost all hope.  I was contacted by a company that claimed they would stop the foreclosure and I had to make monthly payments to them.  Someone suggested that I consult with Damien and he warned me that I was about to be ripped off.  We discussed Chapter 7 or Chapter 13, but decided it was best to go with a loan modification.
Damien took the time to discuss all possible options and advised as to the path that would achieve my goal of getting my debt under control, and start repairing my credit.

Damien has always been there for me.  He was kind enough to sympathize with my financial problems and always worked in finding a solution to better my situation.  Something not all lawyers do.

I am very grateful that I got to keep my house.  This would not have been possible if I had not met Damien.

His intense experience and the many years he has been a bankruptcy attorney make him one of the best bankruptcy attorneys in the Ocala Marion county area. He also speaks fluent Spanish and I was able to refer some of my Hispanic friends to him. They were about to drive all the way to Orlando for an attorney that can help them.

I strongly recommend him and I can't give him more than five stars because they are not available, otherwise I would.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Aaron, I appreciate you taking the time to write your review. I love to hear that my clients were able to achieve their desired result, and that my work was able to help them reach it.

I strive to base my advice on what clients want to achieve. That is why, from the initial consultation, through the end of my representation, I try to listen to my clients needs and goals. I don't think any attorney can properly represent a client if they never truly understood their story, or what they want to achieve. I just don't believe that notes in a computer, from a case manager or a paralegal, are enough to really help an attorney guide someone. So I always make sure I am the person they meet with, and make sure I am accessible throughout the process.

I know what its like being in many of the situations my clients are in, so its not difficult for me to put myself in their shoes. The fact that they are entrusting me to help and guide them out of, most likely the most difficult circumstance they have faced, is never lost on me. I strive to treat every client the same as I would treat my friends and family, and sometimes, as in your case, they end up becoming just that.

Thanks for your support and trust throughout the years and always keeping me in mind when someone needs an attorney.

reviews.text: My husband and I had the pleasure of working with Mr. Aranguren during a difficult time in our lives. He made the process stress free and made sure we understood what was happening every step of the way. He keeps the line of communication open and was there to answer all our questions. I would highly recommend his services!
reviews.ownerResponse: Stephanie, thank you for your kind words.  It was a pleasure working with you and your family.  I make an effort to be accessible to my clients, since I know that many times this is their first time they are facing an issue, so they depend on me to guide them.  I take that responsibility seriously, so it is nice to hear that it is appreciated and my efforts don't go unnoticed.  Thanks again for the kind words, I wish all the best for you and your family.

reviews.text: This is the firm you want to represent you in personal injury cases. They were with us every step of the way, constant communication and we were thrilled with the results.  We have recommended them to everyone we know.

He took the time to explain every step of the process, answered all my questions, and made sure I always felt informed and supported.

Bo handled my case with great attention to detail and worked hard to make sure everything was done correctly. His communication was clear and consistent, which gave me a lot of peace of mind during a difficult time.

Thanks to his dedication and expertise, the process went smoothly and the outcome exceeded my expectations. I highly recommend Briggs law firm to anyone who needs an attorney after a car accident. He is trustworthy, responsive, and genuinely committed to getting the best result for his clients.

The Briggs law firm is truly a diamond in the rough! I was referred by a good friend who Bo had helped. They spoke so highly of the people at the Briggs law firm that I decided to work with them, and I couldn't be more thankful of that decision. I've needed their help a couple times now, and they have gone above and beyond to help us get the best results possible. Bo fights for you, and actually cares about you, your family, and your situations. People like Bo are few and far between. If you are in need for an attorney, for any personal reasons or automobile/Truck accidents, I would recommend Briggs Law Firm.
They won't disappoint... !
10 out of 10

Strongly recommend briggs law firm they went above and beyond on my case was strongly satisfied with the out come thanks Mr bo briggs for everything

As I said before, the next thing I know, I get an email from them as I described.  They were upset because I hadn't called them immediately back  when I've never had personal communication with these people in my life.

So try your luck if you will with these birds. They seem a bit overly aggressive for a first encounter.

Bo and Charles have been amazing.  The Briggs law firm showed care for myself and family after I was in car accident. They are quick to answer phone text or email(I had a lot of questions). Thank Briggs law firm you have done so much for me and my family.

Unbelievable dedication and compassion for his clients. Would highly recommend if you’re looking for someone to listen to you and fight for you 100%

In August 2018 our 26 year old son was killed in an accident.  The driver of the other vehicle (16 years old at the time) caused the accident and there were witnesses at the scene who saw and smelled marijuana in the vehicle.  There were many witnesses as well as video evidence from the gas station at the intersection.  Law enforcement was slow to arrive and failed to gather important evidence.
The loss of our son was devastating.  I found myself trying to navigate the legal system in an effort to bring any amount of justice for my son.  I worked closely with the State Attorney to ensure my son didn't "slip through the cracks".  The judge rejected all efforts to get the results of blood tests performed on the driver.  The Florida Highway Patrol (FHP) conducted their investigation so poorly that the driver didn't receive even a ticket, although two were issued.  The FHP failed to submit the charges in the one year period following my son's death.  There was no justice, no accountability and a very minimal insurance policy.
I then took our son's case to a well known "billboard" law firm.  They spent a few months working my son's case and told me there was nothing they could do outside of the minimal insurance settlement.  In fairness to this law firm, I believe they did a reasonable effort and did not charge me for their time.  Then I took our son's case to another law firm in the area with a similar outcome.  I did the best I could do and reluctantly accepted that any justice for our son was not going to happen.
Then, on the recommendation of our probate lawyer, I met with Randy Briggs.  Randy patiently listened to all the circumstances and agreed to accept my son's case.  We met with Randy on several occasions and each time he methodically laid out a strategy.  He also made it clear that our case would not be easy or quick, but he was up for the challenge.  Randy expertly navigated the legal processes and was able to get the medical records and the sworn testimony of the driver, along with video of the actual accident.  Randy established responsibility of the driver's father for the driver's carelessness.  As a result, we settled with the father and Randy also secured a separate settlement with the driver.
Randy Briggs, along with his team, were the only people capable of bringing any amount of justice for my son.  Randy kept us informed throughout the process and his legal skills are the best.  Randy laid out a plan and spent approximately two years bringing all the pieces together.  Randy achieved justice for our son.
I am very thankful we made this connection with The Briggs Law Firm.  I highly recommend them to anyone needing expert, realistic and honest legal assistance.  Believe me when I say they are different.

I called Briggs law firm because it was right up the road after looking around the web. Having had an auto accident and going to the chiropractor they kept telling me I needed an attorney. Call it luck, fate or whatever it was a very good choice. Everyone on their staff was professional yet personable. They connected us with the best care we needed, made sound recommendations and walked us through everything step by step. I was overwhelmed with emotions when we finally got through the process at the end. For us we had spinal surgeries, not a fun journey but we were cared for in the best way possible, if I have any other issues there's nobody I would rather have represented us! Thank you all.

I like the sincere honesty and kindness. They were very helpful and always explained everything so I could understand. They will answer any questions in a timely manner. I recommend them to everyone who may need a law firm. The staff is awesome as are the attorney. Fees are fair too. They work very hard to settle your case. Cindy T.

When I had an issue that arose as a result of an injury, I called one of the big law firms that I had used for a case several years ago. That firm said that they couldn’t help me with the current case. It wasn’t “big” enough for their standards. I searched for local attorneys and found the Briggs Law Firm. I met with Bo and explained my complicated case to him. He was very straightforward in letting me know how my case would most likely play out. He worked diligently and kept me informed at every turn. I was highly impressed with his professionalism. When a friend of mine needed an attorney to represent her after a car accident, I didn’t hesitate to recommend Bo at the Briggs Law Firm. Give them a call, you won’t be disappointed!

I suffered numerous and very serious injuries and was painfully hospitalized from December 15 through January 6 prior to a lengthy at-home recuperation period. The fortunate aspect of this unfortunate pre-Christmas accident was that we had known Ocala-raised lawyer Randy Briggs for many years. Realizing we needed legal representation, we asked for Randy's recommendation for an accident litigation attorney and learned that he, himself, specialized in this! How fortunate that was and how fortunate it continued to be as Randy professionally counseled us, actively pursued our interests and successfully argued for a substantial settlement on our behalf. We shall always be grateful to this "hometown boy" who developed into an outstanding local attorney, who knows the community and its citizens well. Since then he's never forgotten us--and we definitely have never forgotten him and his legal expertise on our behalf.
---Dr. Thomas L. Weaver

Mr. Randy Briggs and his staff were a blessing to work with. Mr. Randy Briggs was very kind and respectful towards the decisions that my family and I made in regards to any medical care and truly cared about my recovery. Robert (Bo) Briggs, I recommend him and his staff for your legal matters. Very professional, hard working, answers questions, helpful, and very calming through the whole process.

After being involved in a motorcycle accident and sustaining some rather serious injuries, I was at a loss as to my rights and responsibilities. After my accident,  I felt completely overwhelmed by everything. The only thing he wanted us to focus on, was my recovery from the injuries I sustained. Mr. Briggs and his law firm go beyond the call of duty for there clients. I know I would not have received the same amount of personal interaction and outcome if I had chosen a larger law firm. I am beyond pleased and grateful for everything he has done for us . He is certainly an amazing lawyer, who always puts others before himself. We could never thank him enough for his legal services.

Two years ago at an extremely emotional and difficult time our family met with Randy Briggs and felt immediately understood. reviews.text: Almost 10 years ago we went to Marianne Howanitz for help after a car accident. What should have been a slam dunk turned out to be what seemed more like a well oiled machine for everyone involved except the client. The only advice she gave us was to settle for peanuts even though both vehicles involved were ticketed at fault. We have been suffering from this accident since then and it looks like we will be for ever. Do your research and if something doesn’t feel right walk away. I would strongly suggest you check out one of the many attorneys out there who specialize in this field and if they have connections with doctors offices or chiropractors run the other way.
reviews.ownerResponse: Mr. Mendoza:  Due to privacy and ethical issues, I cannot comment on the specifics of any case. I can assure you that every case that is settled or tried by Marianne Howanitz, PA is done so only after a full explanation of the risks and benefits to the client, after all questions and concerns have been answered and with the client’s full understanding and consent.  Clients always have the option to not settle a case, to not take a case to trial and have the option to hire a new attorney if they are not happy.   Once a client consents to a settlement of their claim, it is final and irrevocable.  I was quite surprised by your review, especially since it references representation over a decade ago.  Your review is not in alignment with our firm’s strong commitment to client service and professionalism as evidenced by our reviews.  I wish you and your family the best in the future.

reviews.text: Instead of supporting a home town parade they hire a security guard to bully people off the rocks. We are all adults, it’s not like we would ruin their precious rocks. Any business who can’t figure out how to support their town without bullying doesn’t deserve the towns business. A feeling shared by all the hometown folks sitting on the sidewalk currently.
reviews.ownerResponse: Hi Dannie: I am sorry that you felt compelled to write a one-star review on my law firm website based upon the fact that there was a security guard posted on my firm property during the 2022 Ocala Christmas Parade. Believe me, I wasn’t trying to bully anyone, rather, I was trying to ensure that everyone who may be viewing the parade in front of our office had a safe and enjoyable time. Unfortunately, in years past during the Christmas Parade, the sidewalk between the front of our office and Silver Springs Boulevard gets quite congested and many pedestrians then choose to climb up into our planters and trample our plants and lights. Additionally, children have been permitted to roam and play unattended in our front planters which has resulted in them throwing the rocks from our planters into the sidewalk and street area. I don’t want anyone to get hurt because of the actions of the irresponsible, i.e. deciding to walk through a planter or allowing their children to throw rocks into a crowd. It is because of this that I hired a security guard to make sure this did not happen. I take great pride in being a member of this community where I live, work and have raised my family. I have represented families of the injured in this community for over 20 years and, by hiring a security guard, it was my hope to make sure that there was no damage to property or injury to parade attendees. Happy New Year!

reviews.text: On January 18, 2018 my life changed forever.  As the result of an auto accident I was involved in I was seriously injured, and for the next three years I was treated by doctors, surgeons, and therapists.  I met with Marianne Howanitz and hired her to represent me in my accident case.  Marianne, and staff members April and Corey were by my side every step of the way during my three year ordeal, advising me, comforting me and working hard to bring my case to a close.  After surgeries, procedures and treatments with top providers in my area, Marianne fought hard to settle my case. As a result of her hard work, along with that of her staff, Including April Baxter, I am sitting in my new home, celebrating Thanksgiving . I was able to pay huge medical bills, buy a new car, and for the first time in my life purchase my own home.  I will be able to get the ongoing healthcare that I will need as a result of the accident. To say I am thankful this year for Marianne Howanitz is an understatement. I recommend Marianne and her staff to anyone who needs a tough lawyer who will fight for you as if you were family-for that is how you are treated, like family.

I keep a conflict-checker to make sure that I only meet with one side of a divorce/family law case as required by the Florida Bar. After checking the conflict-checker and my calendar, we show absolutely no record of me or my paralegal ever meeting with you. I simply cannot understand why a person would leave a negative review of me or my firm when they have never met with me for a consultation. And honestly, I have no idea whether or not you're an opposing party trying to publicly defame me using a fictitious name. I suppose your negative feedback is positive testament to my avid, dedicated representation of my clients. Thank you!

Traveling without notice to her clients & would only reach out to her clients when its convenient to her. She sweet talks you but then disregards you all together. Talk about being manipulative. I paid her $14,500 in full & she took on my case to then screw me over in the process. After hiring a totally new law firm I have been told how much my 1st attorney (Madeline) did nothing for me on my case even after she got paid to do her job. “Most attorneys are in it for the money not for the case” she expresses but shes one of them 100%. I even had to file a case with the Florida Bar to get my Money returned. After being assigned to my case for 5 months NOTHING was completed. Talk about people doing things only half way. Attorney Madeline responds to google reviews with no confidentially & unprofessionalism.

Unfortunately the evidence in the court docket shows carelessness on my case from this Attorney.

Profound statements & accusations from the new hired assistant created an escalated situation. The new assistant misled Madeline with false allegations & misled the client (me) with misleading information. If this is how clients are treated, spoken to or responded to by the assistant & by the Attorney herself then Attorney Madeline is not worth the expense in my opinion.

This is simply to inform others of my experience. No need for confidentiality to be broken by an Attorney registered with the Florida Bar in a google review response. Good Luck to all those willing to proceed with Attorney Madeline. Everyone has different experiences.
reviews.ownerResponse: Reply: "Ethics rules prevent me from fully responding to this review however I disagree with its substance.  - “A lawyer’s duty to keep client confidences has few exceptions and in an abundance of caution I do not feel at liberty to respond in a point by point fashion in this forum. I retained Diane when I petitioned for child support back in the mid 2000's. In response, opposing countered for primary residential plus support from me. So, for whatever reason, a simple request for child support from a parent we'd not seen in a decade turned into an absurd custody dispute. (Perhaps she really wanted to earn the hefty retainer I'd paid?) From Diane, all I received was attitude and was then subsequently accused of "coaching my child," re: a GAL interview (which I thought was incredibly odd as I certainly had not; there was nothing to coach, my child barely even knew of the father in the first place.) I had no idea at the time that this accusation was a prelude and guise. Then, Diane vanishes on an out-of-the-country excursion and upon return filed a motion for withdraw. When I inquired regarding said hefty retainer as I certainly couldn't afford another to a new attorney, I received a letter stating that I'd "exhausted my retainer," absent of any itemization. As a result of this attorney's behavior, my child (and I) ended up suffering. I won't go into detail, however even at age 26 now, my child still remembers that misery, as do I. It's my opinion that to accept a retainer and to not complete the work - let alone finish the case - causes a parent undue financial hardship, not to mention stress. It would be prudent for anyone who is seeking counsel to consider your options carefully.

reviews.text: Ridiculously rude staff
reviews.ownerResponse: This review is in error, as Piccin & Glynn has no record of any communication with Ms. Johnson.  At Piccin & Glynn, we uphold the highest standards in communicating with clients and prospective clients. We take pride in our long history of diligently advocating for our clients when they need it most.

reviews.text:
Unprofessional, Unresponsive, and Purely Profit-Driven Experience

I retained Piccin & Glynn after an auto accident. In the beginning, things seemed fine — they were investigating and appeared to be doing their job. But once it became clear that the at-fault driver was likely fraudulent and uninsured, everything changed. Their private investigator even found multiple vehicles with transporter tags linked to defunct businesses at the man’s home, yet after realizing this case wasn’t going to be a “money pit,” their interest and communication dropped off entirely.

From then on, updates stopped, and I had to repeatedly reach out just to find out what was happening. Only after pressing for answers did I discover they had decided to file suit without ever consulting me. When I pointed out how little information I’d been given, I learned just how much they had kept from me during the investigation.

Most of my communication was through Judi, who repeatedly gave vague or incomplete responses and failed to heed constructive criticism even when I specifically asked for clearer updates and transparency. Instead of improving communication, they became even less responsive. Eventually, my point of contact shifted to Leslie, but even then, Attorney John Piccin himself never once replied directly to any of my emails.

When my concerns escalated, they finally scheduled a meeting with Piccin. During that meeting, he decided not to address the raised concern about their communication and the developing issues in my case. Instead, he focused on whether I was still injured and even told me outright that if I was fully healed, he couldn’t help me. That made it crystal clear that his focus was on potential payout — not on actually representing me fully or defending my interests.

Piccin didn’t even know basic, key facts about my case and had to rely on the same staffer I had complained about. That staffer refused to come into the meeting — she just yelled answers from another room, which was completely unprofessional.

After I voiced that I was considering other representation, the firm went totally silent. They refused to provide a letter of consent that would have allowed other attorneys to speak with me about my case. Their only response after that was to inform me that my file was ready for pickup once I terminated them.

In my experience, this firm was disorganized, dismissive, and profit-motivated, not client-focused. Once they realized my case wouldn’t be lucrative, they stopped communicating, ignored my concerns, and retaliated when I tried to move on.

If you value honest communication, professional conduct, and attorneys who actually care about your case beyond the payout, I would strongly recommend looking elsewhere.
reviews.ownerResponse: A lawyer’s duty to keep client confidences has few exceptions, and, in an abundance of caution, I do not feel at liberty to respond in a point-by-point fashion in this forum. The writer’s multiple negative reviews of multiple law firms regarding her one case say more about the writer than the law firms, which remain focused on representing clients in a thorough, honest, and ethical manner.  Piccin & Glynn is proud of the hard work we do every day to help our clients when they need it most.

reviews.text: I waited 5 years for another attorney to settle my case. Mr. Cannon took over and within less than a year he won my case! In all honesty, I was skeptical of him, because of past attorneys. He showed me more respect, professionalism, as well as care for me as a person. More so than any other attorney I have ever had. His office staff was just as incredible as he was. They care about you and your family. They fight to the fullest to make things right. Hands down, would use Mr. Cannon again without a second thought. I have to live the rest of my life in pain and with my disabilities, but Mr. Cannon did everything in his power to make it a bit easier for my family and I. Even after my case was over, he called to check in. That alone made me believe he was the right choice. Most attorneys look at you as a bag of money. If I had to give you any reason not to use Mr. Cannon, I would have to say, you can't use him unless you are in Marion County, FL. During my case, my husband took another job that moved us out of FL just before 2020. Mr. Cannon still kept up with me and updated me on the progress of my case every step of the way. It didn't matter if it was email, phone call, or video chat. Even being thousands of miles away, it didn't stop him from doing all he could. You will NOT regret The Cannon Law Firm!

reviews.text: I hired Attorney Jennifer Darley to reopen an adoption case from 1916 involving my grandmother. My family was trying to get information about my grandmother's biological father. It was a challenge to locate the court file since there was no number assigned to such an old case. Eventually, the Clerk's office located the adoption file. The judge reviewed our petition and examined the court file, but unfortunately the information that we were seeking was not available. While the outcome of the case was disappointing, at least we no longer have to wonder (as we did for many years) if the answer was in the adoption record. I am satisfied with the efforts of Ms Darley and her staff to go the extra mile to help us attempt to solve the mystery.

Darley and her team were hired for a custody/child support case. They barely communicated anything at all, ever, without having to be persistently asked to do so. The case she/her team were hired for missed a deadline (so don't let Jessica tell you "Well we haven't missed a deadline yet", because that is false), and Darley took so long to submit things to the court that even after she was fired and a better lawyer took over, the damage was done and the effect has be substantial.

Save yourself some time and heartache, move on the the next lawyer, your family will thank you.
